# Lumio Headline Variants

**Author:** lumio_copywriter (step 5)
**Produced:** 2026-04-24T03:14:10Z
**Chosen variant:** V2, used on landing hero and final CTA.

## V1, functional

> Capture, summarize, and remember everything you read.

Evaluation: accurate, uninspired. The word "capture" evokes kidnapping, not reading.

## V2, chosen

> **Read smarter. Remember longer.**

Evaluation: keeps the promise on two beats, the second beat underlined in accent amber. Short enough to carry the page, concrete enough to earn trust.

## V3, poetic

> Your reading, alive again.

Evaluation: strong but ambiguous for cold traffic, parks on "alive" which raises the question it should answer.

## V4, direct

> The only reading tool that actually remembers for you.

Evaluation: overpromises, the competitor grid is crowded, "only" will not survive fact-check.

## V5, playful

> Like a librarian in your pocket, but quieter.

Evaluation: memorable, a bit cute for Deep Reader segment. Reserve for social snippets.

## Subhead chosen

> Lumio is the smart reading companion that captures highlights, auto-summarizes long articles, and quietly ties it all together with spaced recall, so the best parts of what you read actually stick.

Evaluation: three clauses, three features, one promise. Rhythm holds at 47 words. Satisfies the "one sentence tells the whole product" Heuristic H-3 from the copywriter playbook.
